description:
Model a 3-meter deep stormwater retention basin with 3:1 sloped embankments in SketchUp, and use Skelion to place flush-mounted solar panels on the south-facing interior slope.

Instructions:
1. Ensure model units are set to meters.
2. Draw a 60m x 30m rectangle on the ground plane.
3. Offset the rectangle exactly 9 meters inward.
4. Use the Move tool to lower the inner face by exactly 3 meters on the negative Blue (Z) axis (this creates the sloped embankments).
5. Select the south-facing interior sloped face (the long embankment on the north side, sloping downward to the south).
6. Open the Skelion plugin dialog.
7. Configure panels for Flush Mount (tilt = 0 degrees relative to surface), Landscape orientation, and default panel dimensions.
8. Insert the panels onto the selected sloped face.
9. Save the completed model to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\basin_solar.skp
